params.name: sort
params.description: Specify sort field.

params.name: direction
params.description: Specify sort direction.

params.name: per_page
params.description: Specify the maximum number of results to return per page.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the next_cursor or prev_cursor from previous responses.

params.name: repository_id
params.description: Filter by repository ID.

params.name: before_full_scan_id
params.description: Filter by before full scan ID.

params.name: after_full_scan_id

items.docs:
Returns a paginated list of all diff scans in an organization.

This endpoint consumes 1 unit of your quota.

This endpoint requires the following org token scopes:
- diff-scans:list

params.name: org_slug
params.description: The slug of the organization

params.name: diff_scan_id
params.description: The ID of the diff scan

params.name: omit_license_details
params.description: Omit license details in the response. This can reduce the size of the response significantly, but will not include license information for the artifacts.

params.name: omit_unchanged
params.description: Omit unchanged artifacts from the response. When set to true, the unchanged field will be set to null.

params.name: cached
params.description: Return cached immutable scan results. When enabled and results are cached, returns the pre-computed scan. When results are not yet cached, returns 202 Accepted and enqueues a background job. Note: When cached=true, the omit_license_details parameter is ignored as cached results always includes license details.

items.docs:

params.description: The ID of the diff scan

items.docs:

params.name: org_slug
params.description: The slug of the organization

params.name: diff_scan_id

params.name: github_installation_id
params.description: The ID of the GitHub installation. This will be used to get the GitHub installation settings. If not provided, the default GitHub installation settings will be used.

items.docs:
Get the dependency overview and dependency alert comments in GitHub flavored markdown for an existing diff scan.

This endpoint consumes 1 unit of your quota.

- diff-scans:list

info.name: Create diff scan from repository HEAD full-scan

params.name: org_slug
params.description: The slug of the organization

params.name: repo_slug
params.description: The slug of the repository

params.name: description
params.description: A description of the diff scan. This will be used in the diff report and can be used to provide context for the changes made.

params.name: external_href
params.description: An external URL to associate with the diff scan. This can be a link to a pull request, issue, or any other relevant resource.

params.name: branch
params.description: The branch name to associate the new full-scan with. Branch names must follow Git branch name rules: be 1–255 characters long; cannot be exactly @;  cannot begin or end with /, ., or .lock; cannot contain "//", "..", or "@{"; and cannot include control characters, spaces, or any of ~^:?*[.

params.name: commit_message
params.description: The commit message to associate the new full-scan with.

params.name: commit_hash
params.description: The commit hash to associate the full-scan with.

params.name: pull_request
params.description: The pull request number to associate the new full-scan with.

params.name: committers
params.description: The committers to associate the new full-scan with. Set query more than once to set multiple committers.

params.name: integration_type
params.description: The integration type to associate the new full-scan with. Defaults to "api" if omitted.

params.name: integration_org_slug
params.description: The integration org slug to associate the new full-scan with. If omitted, the Socket org name will be used. This is used to generate links and badges.

params.name: merge
params.description: Set to true when running a diff between a merged commit and its parent commit in the same branch. Set to false when running diffs in an open PR between unmerged commits.

params.name: workspace
params.description: The workspace of the repository.

items.docs:
Create a diff scan between the repository's current HEAD full scan and a new full scan from uploaded manifest files.
Returns metadata about the diff scan. Once the diff scan is created, fetch the diff scan from
the [api_url](/reference/getDiffScanById) URL to get the contents of the diff.

The maximum number of files you can upload at a time is 5000 and each file can be no bigger than 268 MB.

This endpoint consumes 1 unit of your quota.

This endpoint requires the following org token scopes:

info.name: Create diff scan from full scan IDs

params.name: org_slug
params.description: The slug of the organization

params.name: before
params.description: The ID of the before/base full scan (older)

params.name: after
params.description: The ID of the after/head full scan (newer)

params.name: description
params.description: A description of the diff scan. This will be used in the diff report and can be used to provide context for the changes made.

params.name: external_href
params.description: An external URL to associate with the diff scan. This can be a link to a pull request, issue, or any other relevant resource.

params.name: merge
params.description: Set to true when running a diff between a merged commit and its parent commit in the same branch. Set to false when running diffs in an open PR between unmerged commits.

items.docs:
Create a diff scan from two existing full scan IDs. The full scans must be in the same repository.
Returns metadata about the diff scan. Once the diff scan is created, fetch the diff scan from
the [api_url](/reference/getDiffScanById) URL to get the contents of the diff.

This endpoint consumes 1 unit of your quota.

This endpoint requires the following org token scopes:
      - diff-scans:create
- full-scans:list

params.name: org_slug
params.description: The slug of the organization

items.docs:
Get a list of supported files for full scan generation.
Files are categorized first by environment (e.g. NPM or PyPI), then by name.

Files whose names match the patterns returned by this endpoint can be uploaded for report generation.
Examples of supported filenames include `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, and `yarn.lock`.

This endpoint consumes 1 unit of your quota.

This endpoint requires the following org token scopes:
- No Scopes Required, but authentication is required

params.name: language
params.description: Language for alert metadata

items.docs:
For an array of alert type identifiers, returns metadata for each alert type. Optionally, specify a language via the 'language' query parameter.

This endpoint consumes 1 unit of your quota.
